title: 2026 Waste Management Allowance and Host Fees Increase Notice
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 17, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Suzanne Groneman, Environmental Services Manager
Cynthia Esparza, Director
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: 2026 Waste Management Allowance and Host Fees Increase Notice

On February 27, 2026, Waste Management notified the City of Reno of rate adjustments in accordance with the City of Reno's disposal agreement. The adjustments are based on the Consumer Price Index Fund (CPI) for garbage and trash collection, which increased 5.27% from December 2024 to December 2025. The adjustments herein will take effect on April 1, 2026.

In accordance with Section 3.3 of the agreement, City of Reno allowances will be increased as follows:
Service Previous Allowance New Allowance Service explanation
Collection $ 1,287,891.53 $ 1,355,763.41* Facility and receptacle collection
Transfer Stations $ 1,249,970.47 $ 1,315,843.91 Stead and Sage St. drop-off
Lockwood Landfill $ 60,974.17 $ 64,187.51 Lockwood drop-off
* Additional funding for clean-up boxes is $20,118.32

In accordance with Section 3.6 of the agreement, the host fee paid by Waste Management to the City of Reno will increase from $0.63 to $0.66 per ton.

In accordance with Section 5.2 of the agreement, the following rates are not paid by the public and are paid by Waste Management post-collections, but will also increase:
Current Rate Rate Change New Rate
Compacted Yards $ 17.30 $ 0.91 $ 18.21
Uncompacted Yards $ 13.50 $ 0.71 $ 14.21
Approved Recyclables (tons) $ 65.58 $ 3.46 $ 69.04

CC: Suzanne Groneman
Jorge Ballesteros
Maria Davis
Ali Szabo
Dee Vang

Dan Mognaga
Jonathan Crabtree
Christina Rodriguez
April Puhaida
Sean Banks




Northern Nevada Waste Management
100 Vassar Street
Reno, NV 89502
2/27/2026

Ms. Jackie Bryant
City Manager, City of Reno
P.O. Box 1900
Reno, Nevada 89505

Dear Ms. Bryant:

This is to notify Ms. Jackie Bryant of rate adjustments per the franchise agreements between the City of Reno and WM. Per the Bureau of Labor Statistics the Consumer Price Index-Garbage and Trash Collection for the period of December 2024 to December 2025 increased 5.27%. The following changes will take effect April 1, 2026.

Per Section 3.6 of the Disposal Agreement, the Host Fee WM pays to the City of Reno will increase from $.63 per ton to $.66 per ton for all material passing through transfer stations in Reno.

Per section 5.2 of the Disposal Agreement, solid waste rates per ton will be adjusted as listed below. The solid waste rates are internal rates between the hauling company and post collections. The solid waste rates do not affect the rate paid by the public at the transfer station.

Current Rate Rate Change New Rate
Compacted Yards $17.30 $0.91 $18.21
Uncompacted Yards $13.50 $0.71 $14.21
Approved Recyclables Per Ton $65.58 $3.46 $69.04

Additionally, per section 3.3 E of the Disposal agreement the City of Reno's Disposal allowances will be increased as follows:
Collection Service Allowance $1,355,763.41
Transfer Station Allowance $1,315,843.91
Lockwood Landfill Allowance $64,187.51

Kendra Kostelecky is happy to discuss further if Ms. Jackie Bryant has any questions.

Sincerely,
Kendra Kostelecky
Waste Management

CC: Suzanne Groneman
Jorge Ballesteros
Maria Davis
Ali Szabo
Dee Vang

Dan Mognaga
Jonathan Crabtree
Christina Rodriguez
April Puhaida
Sean Banks




Northern Nevada Waste Management
100 Vassar Street
Reno, NV 89502

fields.authors: Suzanne Groneman, Environmental Services Manager; Cynthia Esparza, Director
fields.tldr: Waste Management notified the City of Reno on February 27, 2026 of rate adjustments under the existing disposal agreement, driven by a 5.27% CPI increase for garbage and trash collection from December 2024 to December 2025. City allowances for collection, transfer stations, and Lockwood Landfill will all increase, and the host fee paid by Waste Management to the City rises from $0.63 to $0.66 per ton. Internal solid waste rates for compacted yards, uncompacted yards, and approved recyclables will also increase, though these do not affect rates paid by the public. All changes take effect April 1, 2026.
fields.key_stats: CPI increase: 5.27% (December 2024–December 2025); Collection allowance: $1,287,891.53 → $1,355,763.41 (plus $20,118.32 for clean-up boxes); Transfer Station allowance: $1,249,970.47 → $1,315,843.91; Lockwood Landfill allowance: $60,974.17 → $64,187.51; Host fee: $0.63 → $0.66 per ton; Compacted Yards rate: $17.30 → $18.21; Uncompacted Yards rate: $13.50 → $14.21; Approved Recyclables rate: $65.58 → $69.04 per ton
fields.key_decisions: No council action is explicitly requested; this is an informational notice of contractual rate adjustments triggered automatically by CPI provisions in the disposal agreement.
fields.action_items: Council and City Manager to be aware that new allowance and rate schedules take effect April 1, 2026; Kendra Kostelecky (Waste Management) is available to answer questions.

people: Cynthia Esparza, Director, Community Engagement & Services
